items.title: Ottawa reverses order to surrender some 'lost Canadian' citizenship certificates
items.summary_auto: Just one week after some "lost Canadians" were told to surrender their new citizenship certificates, a few received letters over the weekend confirming their citizenship claims are valid once again.

items.title: Competition Bureau looking deeper into how Sobeys can control who sells food in your area
items.summary_auto: The Competition Bureau has obtained court orders requiring the parent company of Sobeys to produce documents and testimony for its investigation into property controls, which are agreements that can limit the places where a new grocery store can open.

items.title: Manitoba plans to tackle 'shrinkflation' by requiring grocery stores to post unit prices across province
items.summary_auto: Making it easier for customers to spot "shrinkflation" — through mandatory unit pricing across Manitoba grocery stores — is among the ways the province plans to tackle high food prices.

items.title: Montreal police provide an update on shooting that killed police officer, civilian
Jimmy Kimmel announces longtime Trump adversary Rosie O’Donnell to guest host during two-month hiatus
items.summary_auto: Jimmy Kimmel is taking a two-month break away from his late-night show and has tapped none other than comedian and Donald Trump’s longtime nemesis Rosie O’Donnell as one of several guest hosts taking the reins during his hiatus.

items.title: Live updates: Montreal police provide an update on shooting that killed police officer, civilian
Energy minister plans 'nuclear renaissance' with up to 10 reactors built by 2040
items.summary_auto: The federal government is looking to build up to 10 new nuclear reactors over the next 15 years, with at least one of them outside of Ontario.

items.title: Man, 70, pleads guilty to dangerous driving after crash into Richmond Hill, Ont., daycare that killed toddler last year
items.summary_auto: A man, 70, has pleaded guilty to dangerous driving charges after an SUV he was driving crashed into a daycare north of Toronto last year, killing a toddler and injuring other children and staff members.

items.title: 'No plan, no services, no support': How an Innu teen died after aging out of system
N.L. needs to ‘acknowledge the harms’ it caused with exhibit dispute, says Innu cultural guardian
items.summary_auto: Ahead of a meeting with Premier Tony Wakeham and Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation Minister Lela Evans stemming from the fallout from a cancelled exhibit, Innu Cultural Guardian Jodie Ashini says she wants meaningful action going forward.

items.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo outbreak exceed 1,000, authorities say
items.summary_auto: A total of 100 people have recovered in the outbreak concentrated in the Ituri province since it was declared on May 15, Congo's Ministry of Health said Sunday. At least 365 patients are in hospitals or in isolation, it said.

items.title: Music industry giant Clive Davis, who signed Bruce Springsteen and Whitney Houston, dead at 94
items.summary_auto: Clive Davis, one of the most influential people in the music industry and the record executive who helped bring Bruce Springsteen, Whitney Houston and Aretha Franklin into the spotlight, has died at the age of 94.

items.title: Brady Tkachuk requested trade, Senators GM confirms
items.summary_auto: Ottawa Senators president and general manager Steve Staios confirmed Monday that the team's captain Brady Tkachuk asked to be traded, but did not reveal why.

items.title: U.S. to permit Iranian oil sales, easing decades-old sanctions, as peace talks continue
items.summary_auto: The United States authorized Iranian oil sales on Monday, easing decades-old sanctions as it pushes toward a final peace deal with Tehran in return for commitments on nuclear inspections and free transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Consumer prices rose again in May — but has inflation peaked?
items.summary_auto: Inflation blew past expectations and rose to 3.2 per cent in May. But core inflation remained on target and the energy price shock is starting to relent.

items.title: 2 RCMP officers shot by man in Melville, Sask., police say
items.summary_auto: RCMP say two officers were taken to hospital in Melville, Sask., after being shot while responding to an assault call on Sunday night.

items.title: RCMP say 2 officers shot by man in Melville, Sask.
items.summary_auto: RCMP say two officers were taken to hospital in Melville, Sask., after being shot while responding to an assault call on Sunday night.

items.title: 3 deaths reported in France as much of Europe grapples with extreme heat
items.summary_auto: Three people died due to extreme heat in France and ‌thousands of schools closed or modified timetables as European authorities issued heatwave warnings and forecasters in Britain said temperatures could smash records for June this week.

items.title: Manitoba Court of King's Bench Chief Justice Glenn Joyal nominated to Supreme Court of Canada
items.summary_auto: The chief justice of Manitoba's Court of King's Bench has been nominated to Canada's highest court.

items.title: How Keir Starmer's support evaporated in just 24 months
items.summary_auto: Keir Starmer is out as Britain’s prime minister, but the country’s big problems remain. It's unclear how Andy Burnham plans to navigate a deeply polarized nation that has repeatedly turfed its prime ministers.

items.title: De la Espriella's law-and-order platform wins Colombia vote after earning Trump's support
items.summary_auto: Abelardo de la Espriella, characterized by critics as far right, had earned the endorsement of U.S. President Donald Trump, who had said he would stop illegal immigration and crack down on crime and drugs.

items.title: Deluge of rain leads to flooding, water restrictions in Edmonton and area
items.summary_auto: The Tuesday forecast calls for clear, sunny skies but more rain is in the forecast for the weekend, which could tip the scales to make this June the rainest on record, according to Environment Canada.

items.title: Canada's inflation rate rose to 3.2% in May, driven — again — by high gas prices
items.summary_auto: Canada's annual inflation rate edged up to 3.2 per cent in May, according to Statistics Canada. While gas prices led the overall rate higher, the cost of fresh fruit and vegetables also soared, driving up overall food inflation.

items.title: Woman struggles to find accessible World Cup tickets for her terminally ill husband
items.summary_auto: A woman who raised more than $5,000 to take her terminally ill husband to a FIFA World Cup match in Vancouver says she has been unable to get wheelchair-accessible tickets through FIFA’s accessibility process.

items.title: Delayed remediation at shuttered Edmonton chemical plant raises contamination concerns
items.summary_auto: An environmental enforcement order has been issued against a shuttered chemical plant that converted Edmonton’s residential waste into biofuel. The order was issued because of concerns about abandoned infrastructure and lingering chemical contamination on-site.

items.title: These nuns donated $3M to expand a seniors' home in Ponteix, Sask.
items.summary_auto: The Sisters of Notre-Dame d'Auvergne, who have called Ponteix home for more than a century, are giving back to the community that helped feed them when times were tough.

items.title: Beekeepers working to revolutionize Canadian honey industry with locally-sourced queens
items.summary_auto: A group of Calgary-area apiarists are working to reduce the Canadian beekeeping industry's dependence on foreign-imported queens by instead breeding them themselves, with locally-sourced queens potentially better-equipped to survive Canada's harsh winters.

items.title: Opioid deaths dropped across Canada last year. Alberta's decline was comparatively small
items.summary_auto: Canadian health officials called a 23-per-cent drop in opioid toxicity deaths last year "real progress," which was mirrored by two out of the three provinces that account for most of those deaths. But the third province, Alberta, saw just a small decline, while opioid-related EMS responses jumped.

items.title: Vance says talks with Iranian officials set 'good foundation' for a deal to end war
items.summary_auto: U.S. Vice-President JD Vance on Monday said his lengthy talks with senior Iranian officials in Switzerland created a "good foundation for a successful final deal" as they negotiate bringing a permanent end to the war that the U.S. and Israel began in late February.

items.title: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation, sets out timetable for exit
A white nationalist group visiting Canadian locations is trying to normalize extremism, says a former neo-Nazi
Remember that star that was supposed to explode in 2024? It didn't, but it could still happen soon
items.summary_auto: A new naked-eye star was supposed to grace our skies in 2024, but it never happened. Astronomers say that it's still on the books and could even happen soon.

items.title: Doug Ford’s daily calendar kept secret after changes to Ontario’s FOI law
items.summary_auto: Premier Doug Ford’s daily calendar will now be kept secret from the public following recent changes to Ontario’s freedom of information law — which no longer includes access to records held by ministers and their offices.

items.title: Montréal-Nord family alleges abuse of police power after birthday party pepper spraying
items.summary_auto: With officers at a Montréal-Nord police station under investigation for discrimination and racist acts, a local Black family is speaking out. They have filed a complaint against police alleging excessive force after a backyard birthday party ended in pepper spray and hospitalization.

items.title: Amidst cross-border hostility, celebratory fireworks unite Windsor-Detroit
items.summary_auto: Thousands are expected to line the streets of Windsor, Ont. and Detroit, Mich. to see the 68th annual Ford Fireworks on Monday. But amid cross-border tensions, the shared festivities are taking on a different meaning for some.

items.title: Why the debate about MAID for mental illness still rages and where the conversation is at today
items.summary_auto: The debate over whether Canadians with mental illness should have access to a medically assisted death ramped up this past week raising questions around why it's so heavily scrutinized and who is most impacted.

items.title: Ottawa cut therapy sessions for refugees to 10 hours per year. Now some are pushing back
items.summary_auto: When the federal government introduced supplemental health copayments for asylum seekers and refugee claimants earlier this spring, it also quietly brought in a 10-hour yearly cap on mental health sessions for them, CBC News has learned. Mental health practitioners have been lobbying for a reversal since, with limited success.

items.title: Egypt downs New Zealand 3-1 for first World Cup victory
items.summary_auto: Mohamed Salah scored the go-ahead goal and Egypt went on to defeat New Zealand 3-1 on Sunday night and secured its first-ever World Cup victory.

items.title: Canada solidifies agreement with Australia to buy Arctic Over-the-Horizon Radar system
items.summary_auto: Canada has taken the next step toward acquiring a highly sophisticated, long-range radar system to monitor the Arctic.

items.title: The U.S. says it's open. Iran says it's closed. The Strait of Hormuz remains precarious
items.summary_auto: Is the Strait of Hormuz open or closed? Comments from American and Iranian leaders tell conflicting stories on the critical waterway, with the U.S. saying it remains open to shipping traffic and Iran saying it's been closed again.

items.title: Abelardo De La Espriella wins Colombia election, continuing rightward trend in Latin America
items.summary_auto: Colombian right-wing candidate Abelardo De La Espriella has clinched a narrow victory in Sunday's presidential election, according to an initial ballot count, as voters bet on his promise of a crackdown on crime and a stronger economy.
